title: M Is For The Many Things She Gave Me

jokes.text: Well, maybe it would have worked if you had taken her too.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil explaining he spent money on 50 dinners trying to win over Janice
jokes.punchline: Will points out Phil only paid for the dinners, not for taking Janice along
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay with perfect timing. The misdirection works well and it's economically delivered.

jokes.text: Sorry, it was just a little collegiate humor.
jokes.setup: Will delivers a clever joke
jokes.punchline: He immediately apologizes and categorizes his own humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the joke itself is character-appropriate for Will's self-awareness.

jokes.text: Finally, after two years... I realized that Janice wasn't gonna have me, so I settled for Vivian. With Vivian. With Vivian!
jokes.setup: Phil trying to explain his romantic history
jokes.punchline: His horrible word choice and desperate attempts to correct himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with escalating panic. Phil's character would absolutely make this mistake and compound it.

jokes.text: Geoffrey, prepare Mr. Banks' couch.
jokes.setup: Phil's disastrous 'settled for Vivian' comment
jokes.punchline: Viv's ice-cold dismissal relegating him to the couch
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered comeback with maximum economy. The formality makes it even more devastating.

jokes.text: It's in your tie drawer next to the Pringles.
jokes.setup: Phil asking where his purple tie is
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey matter-of-factly reveals Phil keeps snacks in his tie drawer
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that perfectly captures Phil's food obsession and Geoffrey's dry delivery style.

jokes.text: Least that's better than the Kit Kat he keep with his drawers.
jokes.setup: The Pringles in the tie drawer revelation
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey escalates with an even more inappropriate food hiding place
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds on the previous joke, though slightly less impactful than the setup.

jokes.text: My horoscope was right. This is my lucky day.
jokes.setup: Someone is in a coma and Hilary might get their job
jokes.punchline: Hilary celebrates this as her 'lucky day' per her horoscope
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Hilary's self-absorption and lack of empathy. Dark but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Maybe science can just come get her brain now.
jokes.setup: Hilary's oblivious comment about minority favoritism
jokes.punchline: Will suggests donating her brain to science since she's clearly not using it
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good economy. Will's role as the voice of reason works well here.

jokes.text: She's 18. - Good answer.
jokes.setup: Will asking about the daughter's age in a leading way
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate approval of the age being in his target range
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff that establishes Will's romantic interest efficiently.

jokes.text: Men.
jokes.setup: Will's obvious interest in the 18-year-old daughter
jokes.punchline: Viv's exasperated one-word summary
jokes.explanation: Maximum economy but predictable. Gets the job done without being memorable.

jokes.text: Well, number one, because I don't be saying stuff like 'opt.'
jokes.setup: Carlton asking why the daughter would choose Will over him
jokes.punchline: Will mocks Carlton's formal vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect character differentiation between Will's street smarts and Carlton's pretension.

jokes.text: Number two, because Geoffrey still has to cut up your food.
jokes.setup: Will's first reason why he'd win over Carlton
jokes.punchline: Escalates by infantilizing Carlton with the food-cutting detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds on the previous insult with a vivid, embarrassing detail.

jokes.text: With all due respect, young sir... I've seen you in action with women, and quite frankly, you're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking Geoffrey why he'd bet against him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's brutally honest assessment delivered with formal politeness
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's formal politeness makes the insult even more devastating. Perfect character voice and structure.

jokes.text: Oh, you were shaving your nose? Okay.
jokes.setup: Phil claiming he cut himself shaving to explain his injured nose
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan observation about the anatomical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logic joke that calls out Phil's obvious lie.

jokes.text: Damn! - Damn!
jokes.setup: Wendy enters and is presumably very attractive
jokes.punchline: Both Will and Carlton have the exact same reaction simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that works through timing and unison. Predictable but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, Phil, please stop. - Yes, Phil, please stop.
jokes.setup: Phil complimenting Janice about looking good in a gas mask
jokes.punchline: Both women saying the exact same thing but with opposite meanings
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure showing the tension between the women through identical words with opposite intent.

jokes.text: and they preempt your favorite programs. I remember during Desert Storm, trying to find Knots Landing...
jokes.setup: Discussing being against war for serious reasons
jokes.punchline: Hilary's opposition is based on TV schedule disruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - takes a serious topic and makes it about her trivial concerns.

jokes.text: Perhaps after dinner... we can go for a drive and talk about it in depth.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentioning his theory about regularity/prunes
jokes.punchline: Carlton wanting to discuss bowel movements in detail as a date activity
jokes.explanation: Carlton's complete social obliviousness makes this appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I said I'll pay you when I get out!
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably being arrested or detained
jokes.punchline: His indignant protest about payment from jail/custody
jokes.explanation: Implies Carlton got arrested on his date, which is absurd but fits his track record.

jokes.text: Oh, the Arthur Murray Dance Studios. - Tango?
jokes.setup: Wendy asking where Will learned to dance so well
jokes.punchline: Will claims formal dance lessons, then immediately proves it
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection that subverts expectations about Will's dance background.

jokes.text: That's fine, that's fine.
jokes.setup: Wendy saying Will is moving too fast for her
jokes.punchline: Will's obvious disappointment masked as acceptance
jokes.explanation: Will trying to play it cool when clearly disappointed - relatable awkwardness

jokes.text: Well, you know, it was kind of like Shaq picked up the pool house... and he was shaking it, you know, like them little snow globes.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Robertson asking Will to describe the earthquake
jokes.punchline: Will's vivid, street-smart comparison using Shaq and snow globes
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both vivid and character-appropriate for Will's speech patterns.

jokes.text: Was that baby Nicky? - No, no, baby Carlton.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning hearing crying during the earthquake
jokes.punchline: The crying was from Carlton, not the actual baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff that reinforces Carlton's immaturity. The 'baby Carlton' nickname is gold.

jokes.text: Well, he's kind of like a 10-year-old trapped in a 12-year-old's body.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Robertson noting Carlton isn't mature
jokes.punchline: Will's description making Carlton sound even younger than expected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - instead of being trapped in a child's body, he's a child trapped in an only slightly older child's body.

jokes.text: And frankly, I'm a little uncomfortable with this higher level of comfort.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his discomfort with Mrs. Robertson changing clothes
jokes.punchline: The wordplay on 'comfortable' and 'comfort'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Will trying to be diplomatic while expressing discomfort.

jokes.text: Listen, my uncle has a fat problem. I should be more sensitive.
jokes.setup: Will apologizing for his assumptions about Mrs. Robertson
jokes.punchline: His 'sensitivity' involves mentioning Uncle Phil's weight issues
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt to be sensitive actually makes things worse by bringing up Phil's weight.

jokes.text: There's a picture right there of you with no bathing suit top on!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Robertson showing Will innocent vacation photos
jokes.punchline: Will's shocked discovery of a topless photo
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom escalation that moves the seduction plot forward with Will's obvious discomfort.

jokes.text: Well, you know what? I'm gonna run down to the lobby, get some of them free brochures, you know.
jokes.setup: Will trying to escape the uncomfortable situation
jokes.punchline: His transparent excuse about getting hotel brochures
jokes.explanation: Will's panic creates a believably awkward excuse that shows his good character.

jokes.text: No, no, no. They was in two separate rooms, and I wasn't in either one.
jokes.setup: Will defending himself to Uncle Phil about the night before
jokes.punchline: His over-specific denial that actually implies guilt
jokes.explanation: Will's over-explanation makes him sound more guilty. Good escalation of his panic.

jokes.text: You're over 5 feet, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Will's height requirement for conversation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's uncertain self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Carlton's insecurity about his height makes this callback work well.

jokes.text: See, now, Cleopatra Jones, see, she got that karate stuff, right? She got some sweet moves on her. Plus, she be packing heat. But Grace Jones, she just scary.
jokes.setup: Wendy asking who would win between Cleopatra Jones and Grace Jones
jokes.punchline: Will's detailed analysis ending with Grace Jones being 'just scary'
jokes.explanation: Will's serious analysis of a ridiculous question, with 'just scary' being the perfect punchline.

jokes.text: because, you know, my Uncle Phil likes to eat while he cooks... and might not be nothing left for nobody else, you know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he needs to get food quickly
jokes.punchline: The implication that Phil eats all the food while cooking
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Phil's eating habits delivered in Will's natural voice.

jokes.text: How would it make me look?
jokes.setup: Will asking if Mrs. Robertson wants her daughter to find out about their encounter
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Robertson only caring about her own reputation, not morality
jokes.explanation: Shows Mrs. Robertson's selfishness - she's not ashamed of what she did, just worried about appearances

jokes.text: You know, I still have that halter-top.
jokes.setup: Phil reminiscing about Janice's outfit from decades ago
jokes.punchline: Janice's suggestive comment about still having the item
jokes.explanation: Escalates the flirtation and creates more tension with Viv present.

jokes.text: Saint Bernards are friendly. But even they don't slobber as much as you.
jokes.setup: Phil defending himself as just being friendly
jokes.punchline: Viv's comparison to slobbering dogs
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that's both insulting and vivid. Perfect structure and delivery.

jokes.text: Well, she ain't get away from me.
jokes.setup: Phil saying Janice was the one that got away
jokes.punchline: Will's ominous correction implying he succeeded where Phil failed
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that sets up the big revelation. Economy and impact are strong.

jokes.text: You slept with Janice!
jokes.setup: Will's long, rambling explanation of the previous night
jokes.punchline: Phil's shocked realization and accusation
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that lands with impact. Phil's outrage is perfectly played.

jokes.text: Ashley, go to your room. - Oh, I love this!
jokes.setup: Phil trying to protect Ashley from adult drama
jokes.punchline: Ashley's excitement about the family scandal
jokes.explanation: Ashley's enthusiasm for family drama perfectly captures teenage perspective on adult problems.

jokes.text: Have you ever seen your mother naked?
jokes.setup: Will trying to deflect blame and explain his discomfort
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate question to Wendy
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment where Will makes everything worse with an incredibly inappropriate question.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, go blame it on me!
jokes.setup: Everyone rightfully blaming Will for the situation
jokes.punchline: Will's indignant protest as if he's being unfairly accused
jokes.explanation: Will's complete lack of self-awareness as he protests being blamed for what he actually did.

jokes.text: You can sleep on your couch.
jokes.setup: Phil asking if he doesn't have to sleep on the chaise lounge
jokes.punchline: Vivian's 'generous' offer to let him sleep on his own couch
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening - Phil's punishment continues with minimal mercy from Vivian

jokes.text: From now on, I'm only dating girls with ugly moms.
jokes.setup: Will saying he learned his lesson from the experience
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong takeaway focusing on avoiding temptation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will learned the wrong lesson. Highly quotable and memorable.
